data.change_summary: Fourteenth remediation round addressing 3 amendments (2 implemented, 1 skipped): (1) Replaced `request.timeout + Duration::from_secs(30)` with `request.timeout.saturating_add(Duration::from_secs(30))` in service.rs to eliminate a theoretical panic on Duration overflow. (2) Process group kill skipped — requires unsafe code which the crate forbids via #![forbid(unsafe_code)]; documented since CR3. (3) Fixed backoff window stranding: before the retry backoff sleep, the engine now persists a Failed snapshot with interrupted_run preserved, so a crash or kill during the 5-60s backoff window leaves the project in a resumable state instead of stranded in Running (which `run resume` refuses to recover). The next loop iteration re-sets Running at the top of the stage dispatch.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel. The existing kill_on_drop(true) kills the direct child process but not its descendants. This limitation has been documented since CR3 and applies to all kill paths (timeout, cancel, and normal teardown).

The pre-backoff Failed snapshot transition writes to disk on every retry attempt with non-zero backoff. This adds one extra filesystem write per retry (5 writes in worst case before exhaustion). The write is best-effort (errors are silently ignored) and uses the same run_snapshot_write mechanism already called at the top of each iteration, so the overhead is negligible.

If the process is killed after the backoff completes but before the next loop iteration re-sets Running (a very narrow window between lines 4661 and 4472), the snapshot will still be in Failed state — which is the correct resumable state, so this is safe.
The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal.

steps.description: Replaced request.timeout + Duration::from_secs(30) with request.timeout.saturating_add(Duration::from_secs(30)) in service.rs:210 to prevent a panic on Duration overflow. While practically unlikely with real timeout values, this eliminates a theoretical panic path in production code with a single-method-call change.

Process group kill for subprocess trees: skipped. The crate uses #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe. std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. This has been a documented known limitation since CR3. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed.

Fixed backoff window stranding in engine.rs:4619-4660: before the retry backoff sleep, the engine now (a) preserves the stage resolution snapshot, (b) calls preserve_interrupted_run to save the active_run to interrupted_run, (c) transitions status to RunStatus::Failed, (d) clears active_run, (e) sets a descriptive status_summary ('retrying {stage}: backoff Ns before attempt M'), and (f) writes the snapshot to disk. This mirrors the state transitions in fail_run() but without journaling a run_failed event, since the run is not actually failed — it's just making the snapshot resumable during the sleep window. The next loop iteration re-sets Running at line 4472.
Verified all 683 tests pass, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
service.rs:210 now uses saturating_add — Duration::MAX + 30s saturates to Duration::MAX instead of panicking

Engine retry backoff path: snapshot transitions Running → Failed before sleep, preserving interrupted_run for resume. Status summary clearly indicates the retry state ('retrying plan_and_implement: backoff 5s before attempt 2')
The Failed snapshot during backoff satisfies the resume check at engine.rs:1148 (RunStatus::Failed is accepted) and the active_run check at engine.rs:1166 (active_run is None)

All 72 workflow_engine_test tests pass with --features test-stub: retry exhaustion, resume-from-failed, cancellation during backoff, and preflight failure tests are all unaffected because test backoff uses start_paused=true which resolves sleeps instantly, leaving no window for the snapshot state to be observed
All 7 tmux_adapter_test, all process_backend_test, all retry_policy_test tests pass — changes are confined to service.rs and engine.rs
